title: Yet Satisfiability Again!
description: Alice recently started to work for a hardware design company and as a part of her job, she needs to identify defects in fabricated integrated circuits. An approach for identifying these defects boils down to solving a satisfiability instance. She needs your help to write a program to do this task.
input: The first line of input contains a single integer, not more than 5, indicating the number of test cases to follow. The first line of each test case contains two integers n and m where 1 ≤ n ≤ 20 indicates the number of variables and 1 ≤ m ≤ 100 indicates the number of clauses. Then, m lines follow corresponding to each clause. Each clause is a disjunction of literals in the form Xi or ∼Xi for some 1 ≤ i ≤ n, where ∼Xi indicates the negation of the literal Xi. The “or” operator is denoted by a ‘v’ character and is seperated from literals with a single space.
output: For each test case, display satisfiable on a single line if there is a satisfiable assignment; otherwise display unsatisfiable.

samples.input:
2
3 3
X1 v X2
~X1
~X2 v X3
3 5
X1 v X2 v X3
X1 v ~X2
X2 v ~X3
X3 v ~X1
~X1 v ~X2 v ~X3

source: , ICPC, Regionals, North America, Rocky Mountain Regional, 2014 Rocky Mountain Regional Contest
